findings.issue: The opening framing centers on a transition narrative ('transitioning from data analysis to full-stack development') instead of a direct role/domain anchor, so the first scan reads as career-change rather than immediate SWE fit.
findings.fix: Rewrite the first sentence to start with the target role and domain, for example: 'Full-stack SWE focused on internal platforms and workflow automation in Java, TypeScript, Node.js, Python, and Go,' then move the transition story later in the summary.

findings.field: TikTok project: Security Knowledge Retrieval Assistant, bullet 1
findings.issue: AWS Bedrock appears in the body but is not listed in Skills, so the body and Skills are not fully aligned on the actual stack used.
findings.fix: Add AWS Bedrock to the relevant Skills category, or reword the bullet to a broader cloud-service description if you want to keep the Skills section narrower.

revision_priority: [MUST] Rewrite the opening Summary sentence to lead with a clear full-stack SWE role/domain anchor instead of the transition narrative.
[MUST] Add AWS Bedrock to Skills, or soften the project wording so the stack in the body matches the stack list.
[SHOULD] If you have one more concrete scope cue, add it to the first bullet of the newest role to strengthen the Google Cloud platform fit.
[NICE] Keep the current structure and quantification style; the resume is otherwise internally coherent.
raw_json.revision_instructions: [MUST] Rewrite Summary sentence 1 so it starts with a direct role/domain anchor (for example, 'Full-stack SWE focused on internal platforms and workflow automation...') and keep the transition story as a secondary point. [MUST] Add AWS Bedrock to Skills under Cloud, or rephrase the TikTok project bullet so the body does not introduce a tool that the Skills section omits. [SHOULD] If available, add one explicit ownership/scope cue to the first bullet of the newest role to better support the Google Cloud full-stack target. [NICE] Leave the rest of the structure intact; the resume is otherwise coherent and ATS-friendly.
